name: Banh Pho Bo (Beef Noodle Soup)
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds Beef & chicken bones, meaty combination
1/2 pound Beef sirloin, sliced very thin across grain, bite-sized
8 ounces Rice sticks, soaked in hot water for 30 minutes, then drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice 2 of the onions into 1/4 inch slices.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a frying pan. Add the sliced onion and cook, stirring until the outside has browned. Remove and drain.
3. Slice the remaining onion into paper-thin slices and set aside.
4. Rinse the bones and place them in a stockpot with cold water. Bring slowly to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered. For a clear broth, skim off foam after 10-15 minutes.
5. After 10-15 minutes, add the browned onion, ginger, carrots, cinnamon, cardamom, star anise, cloves, garlic, and peppercorns. Bring to a boil. Simmer the stock, partially covered for 6 to 12 hours, skimming regularly.
6. If necessary, add more water to keep the bones covered. Strain the stock, skim off fat, and discard any remaining solids.
7. At serving time, arrange sliced beef on a platter. Garnish with reserved white and green onion slices.
8. On another platter, arrange bean sprouts, chopped cilantro, lime wedges.
9. Heat the rice sticks in boiling water until heated through. Drain and place equal portions in each soup bowl.
10. Cover to keep warm. Heat beef stock to a boil. Season with fish sauce and pepper. Pour into a soup tureen or chafing dish.
11. At the table, place the soup on a portable warmer to keep hot. Offer each guest a bowl of warm rice noodles.
12. Each diner adds some beef and onion slices to their bowl. Ladle the hot stock over the meat, stirring to cook it. Add bean sprouts, cilantro, chiles, and lime wedges to taste. Enjoy with chopsticks and a soup spoon.
13. Optional: Pass fresh basil leaves, additional cilantro, chilies, fish sauce, and ground peanuts at the table for guests to customize their bowls.
